post.summary:
PreviewGrant checks what permissions a subject would receive if granted
a given role on a resource, without actually creating the grant.

ListResourceAccess returns all subjects that have effective permissions
on a given resource, including inherited access.

ListSubjectEntitlements returns all effective permissions for a subject
across all resources, including inherited permissions from groups,
parent resources, zone-level, and platform-level grants.

resource.description: resource is the resource to query access for.

subjectType.description: subject_type is optional to filter by subject type (e.g., "user", "group").

subject.description: subject is the subject to query entitlements for.

resourceType.description: resource_type optionally filters to a specific resource type.

pageSize.description: page_size controls the maximum number of entitlements to return.

roleKey.description: role_key identifies the role template to preview.

subject.description: subject is the subject that would receive the grant.

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

id.description:
id is a unique identifier for this entitlement entry.
For direct grants, this is the grant_id.
For inherited entitlements, this is "inherited:{hash}".

subject.description: subject is the subject (user, group, service account) that holds this entitlement.

resource.description: resource is the resource this entitlement applies to.

roleKey.description: role_key identifies the role template that defines the permissions.

permissions.description: permissions is the resolved list of permission strings from the role template.

sourceType.description: source_type indicates how this entitlement was derived.

sourceSubject.description:
source_subject points to the entity that actually holds the grant.
For GROUP_GRANT, this is the group. For DIRECT_GRANT, this is the subject itself.

sourceResource.description:
source_resource points to the resource that actually has the grant.
For PARENT_INHERITANCE, this is the parent resource.
For ORG_INHERITANCE, this is the zone.

grantId.description:
grant_id is the ID of the grant record that can be revoked.
Empty for inherited entitlements that cannot be revoked here.

revocableHere.description:
revocable_here indicates whether this entitlement can be revoked from
the current context. Only DIRECT_GRANT entitlements are revocable here.

expiresAt.description: expires_at is the expiration time of the grant, if any.

consistencyToken.description: consistency_token is an opaque token for consistency checking.

v1Entitlement.description:
Entitlement represents a single effective permission entry for a subject
on a resource, with its derivation source.

v1EntitlementSourceType.description:
EntitlementSourceType describes how an entitlement was derived.

 - DIRECT_GRANT: DIRECT_GRANT: A direct grant record exists in Postgres (iam_grants).
 - GROUP_GRANT: GROUP_GRANT: The subject inherits via group membership.
 - PARENT_INHERITANCE: PARENT_INHERITANCE: The subject has permissions on a parent resource.
 - ORG_INHERITANCE: ORG_INHERITANCE: The subject has zone-level (org) permissions.
 - PLATFORM_INHERITANCE: PLATFORM_INHERITANCE: The subject has platform-level permissions.

entitlements.description: entitlements is the list of subjects with effective access to the resource.

totalSize.description: total_size is the total number of entitlements matching the query.

entitlements.description: entitlements is the list of effective permissions for the subject.

totalSize.description: total_size is the total number of entitlements matching the query.

permissions.description: permissions is the list of permissions the subject would gain.

alreadyGranted.description: already_granted indicates if the subject already has this grant.

existingGrantId.description: existing_grant_id is the ID of the existing grant if already_granted is true.
